query.sentence: Identify non fluoropolymer thermoplastics that can withstand severe thermal shock, specifically direct immersion into liquid nitrogen (-196 °C) and maintain structural integrity (no cracking, embrittlement, or catastrophic failure).

properties: High toughness and ductility at cryogenic temperatures (≤ -196 °C)
Low coefficient of thermal expansion (< 50 ppm/K)
Good dimensional stability under rapid temperature change
Excellent chemical resistance to liquid nitrogen
Processability by conventional melt processing (injection, compression, extrusion)
constraints: Must not be a fluoropolymer (e.g., PTFE, PFA)
Must survive direct immersion in liquid nitrogen without cracking or embrittlement
Must maintain structural integrity during severe thermal shock
Must be manufacturable at laboratory scale with standard equipment

final_candidate.justification: PEEK retains >80 % of its room‑temperature tensile strength at –196 °C, exhibits a low CTE (~45 ppm/K), and is widely documented for cryogenic applications such as vacuum chambers and structural components. Its amorphous nature allows it to remain ductile rather than brittle when exposed to liquid nitrogen, meeting the severe thermal shock requirement while remaining non‑fluoropolymer.

process_recipe.description: Dry PEEK pellets to remove moisture before melt processing.
Melt and inject PEEK into the mold.
process_recipe.conditions: 360 °C melt temperature, hold 5 min; injection pressure 80 MPa; mold temperature 20–25 °C.

process_recipe.description: Cool the molded part and demold.
process_recipe.conditions: Controlled cooling to room temperature over 10–15 min; release pressure gradually.
